name: Monasterio de Piedra and Castillo de
name_original: Monasterio de Piedra y Castillo de Monterde — circular desde Nuévalos
description: Cycle 14.6 miles (23.5 km) from Nuévalos to explore Monasterio de Piedra's waterfalls and the historic Castillo de Monterde.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Nuévalos traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the Piedra River and its dramatic geological formations. The region features the verdant Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, known for its waterfalls and grottoes, alongside the expansive La Tranquera Reservoir. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from riverside paths to routes through charming villages and areas with significant elevation changes. This blend of natural beauty and cultural points of interest defines the cycling experience. Best touring cycling routes around Nuévalos The most popular touring cycling route is Monasterio de Piedra and Castillo de, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views of the Monasterio de Piedra and leads to Castillo de Monterde. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Jaraba - Calmarza Loop via GR 24, a difficult 19.4 miles (31.2 km) path. This route explores dramatic canyons and connects the villages of Jaraba and Calmarza. Local touring cyclists also love the Ibdes – La Caprichosa Waterfall of Ibdes loop from Monasterio de Piedra, a 26.8 miles (43.1 km) trail leading through the Monasterio de Piedra area and past La Caprichosa Waterfall, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Nuévalos is defined by the Piedra River's unique geological formations, the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, and the La Tranquera Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging excursions. The routes in Nuévalos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nuévalos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nuévalos?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes in the Nuévalos area, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Nuévalos?
A: The terrain around Nuévalos is quite varied, characterized by the Piedra River and its unique geological formations. You'll find everything from riverside paths and routes through charming villages to areas with significant elevation changes and dramatic canyons. The region includes the lush Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park and the expansive La Tranquera Reservoir, offering a mix of paved and unpaved segments.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Nuévalos?
A: While many routes in Nuévalos are rated as difficult, there are options for those seeking a more moderate experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993372931/monasterio-de-piedra-and-castillo-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra and Castillo de&lt;/a&gt; route is classified as moderate and offers beautiful scenery over 14.6 miles (23.5 km), which might be suitable for more experienced families or beginners looking for a challenge.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Nuévalos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Nuévalos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 9 reviews. Over 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique natural beauty, such as the waterfalls and reservoirs, and the opportunity to visit historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls in the Nuévalos area?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its waterfalls! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31496944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibdes – La Caprichosa Waterfall of Ibdes loop from Monasterio de Piedra&lt;/a&gt; route will take you past the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caprichosa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, which is accessible via several routes, is home to numerous cascades, including the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I visit historical sites or castles while cycling near Nuévalos?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993372931/monasterio-de-piedra-and-castillo-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra and Castillo de&lt;/a&gt; route leads to the historic Castillo de Monterde. You can also explore the area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Monastery Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, a 12th-century Cistercian monastery, which is a major draw for its cultural heritage.

Q: Which touring cycling routes offer the best views or natural landscapes?
A: Many routes offer spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993372931/monasterio-de-piedra-and-castillo-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra and Castillo de&lt;/a&gt; route provides scenic views of the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park. Routes around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tranquera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, such as those passing by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tranquera Reservoir from Cerro de Somé&lt;/a&gt;, are excellent for photography and birdwatching. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993043187/jaraba-calmarza-loop-via-gr-24" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaraba - Calmarza Loop via GR 24&lt;/a&gt; explores dramatic canyons, offering a different kind of breathtaking landscape.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Nuévalos?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993372931/monasterio-de-piedra-and-castillo-de" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra and Castillo de&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 14.6-mile (23.5 km) circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993043187/jaraba-calmarza-loop-via-gr-24" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaraba - Calmarza Loop via GR 24&lt;/a&gt;, which is a more challenging 19.4-mile (31.2 km) path.

Q: What are some options for more challenging or advanced touring cycling routes?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993043187/jaraba-calmarza-loop-via-gr-24" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaraba - Calmarza Loop via GR 24&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.4-mile (31.2 km) route exploring dramatic canyons. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31880977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cetina Castle – La Caprichosa Waterfall of Ibdes loop from Nuévalos&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 31 miles (50 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Nuévalos?
A: Nuévalos offers cycling opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, with lush landscapes and comfortable riding conditions. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter cycling is possible, but checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the La Tranquera Reservoir is a prominent feature in the area. Several routes offer views of this expansive body of water, which is ideal for photography and birdwatching. You can find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5850147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tranquera Reservoir: Bridge and Tunnels&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tranquera Reservoir from Cerro de Somé&lt;/a&gt; along various paths.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available from Nuévalos?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31880977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cetina Castle – La Caprichosa Waterfall of Ibdes loop from Nuévalos&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 31 miles (50 km) and takes approximately 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route offers a significant workout with considerable elevation gain.
tips: As we pass through Monterde, we cannot miss the town's Castle located on top of the rocky hill where the remains of the square-plan tower that was part of a medieval fortress located in the Aragonese town are preserved. At present it is protected as an archaeological zone.

We are in the Monasterio de la Piedra Natural Park (not free), an idyllic place for nature lovers where we will find waterfalls, lakes, caves, beautiful walkways and more. The building itself is an old restored Cistercian monastery from the 13th century, where we can see the transition from Romanesque to Cistercian Gothic and where its spectacular cloister stands out. It is important to check prices and schedules on the web: monasteriopiedra.com

Nice views of Nuévalos and the Tranquera reservoir in the background, from the road reaching the town. If we are lucky enough to see the reservoir with its well-charged water reserves, we will see its spectacular emerald blue color. We will find fishermen, people bathing and a lot of fauna.

faqs.question: Where does this circular cycling route start?
faqs.answer: This circular touring cycling route starts and ends in Nuévalos. You can find parking in the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The komoot data suggests this specific route takes approximately 1 hour and 51 minutes to complete. However, depending on your pace, stops at points of interest, and fitness level, it could take longer, potentially up to five hours.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate and includes elevation changes. Be prepared for some unpaved and difficult segments, which means it might be better suited for gravel bikes or mountain bikes rather than traditional touring bicycles with narrow tires. The Monasterio de Piedra park itself has varied terrain with slopes and stairs, though the cycling route primarily circles it.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. Key highlights include the spectacular Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall within the natural park, the historic Monterde Castle ruins, and a scenic View of Nuévalos and Tranquera Reservoir.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the arid Aragonese landscape, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter, making for a more comfortable experience.

faqs.question: Are there any entrance fees or permits required for this route, especially for the Monasterio de Piedra?
faqs.answer: While cycling the route itself doesn't require a permit, accessing the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park and its waterfalls typically requires an entrance fee. It's advisable to check their official website for current pricing and opening hours if you plan to enter the park on foot.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The cycling route itself is generally accessible with dogs, but if you plan to enter the Monasterio de Piedra Natural Park, it's important to check their specific regulations regarding dogs, as many natural parks have restrictions or require dogs to be on a leash.

faqs.question: Is this a family-friendly cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate due to its elevation changes and some difficult, unpaved segments. While older, experienced cycling families might enjoy it, it may be challenging for very young children or those new to touring cycling, especially on the unpaved sections. The Monasterio de Piedra park itself has areas with stairs and slopes, making it less accessible for strollers or very small children.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Due to the presence of 'unpaved and difficult segments,' a gravel bike or a mountain bike would be better suited for this route than a traditional touring bicycle with narrow tires. These bikes offer better traction and comfort on varied terrain.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: The route does not significantly intersect with other named official trails. It briefly overlaps with a small section of the 'Carretera Antigua' for about 3% of its length.

faqs.question: Can I visit Castillo de Monterde?
faqs.answer: The Castillo de Monterde is a medieval fortress, now largely in ruins, situated atop a rocky outcrop. While you can cycle to the town of Monterde and view the castle from the outside, specific visitor access to the ruins themselves may be limited. It offers a historical counterpoint to the natural beauty of the Monasterio de Piedra.

waypoints.name: View of Nuévalos and Tranquera Reservoir
